codeReview.summary: Implementation matches the feature scope: CSV parsing is centralized in `backend::load_csv_workflow`, Axum/Tauri load entrypoints delegate to it, direct `csv_loader::` calls were removed from those transport layers, and integration tests cover both bytes and file-path sources.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill should explicitly warn that `cargo test <name> -- --exact` can silently match zero tests for plain `#[test]` functions in `src-tauri`, which can give false confidence for targeted checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: In /Users/vriesd/.factory/missions/8fa2796a-8b48-4fd7-a205-fbe53ec6442c/handoffs/2026-04-18T02-11-28-070Z__m5-csv-parsing-behind-backend-workflow__76171671-3420-40bb-b510-d8445b17bfdc.json, verification.commandsRun[1] notes the targeted `src-tauri` name filter matched zero tests; the same point appears in handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges.

summary: Pass: the commit fulfills the m5 CSV parsing consolidation requirement and keeps transport wrappers thin while sharing backend parsing/error handling.
